name: chip
description: A small rounded tag, optionally removable: the Filament active-filter pill / shadcn Badge-with-dismiss. Same variant token vocabulary as badge (default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link/neutral/success/warning/danger/info). Size scale (sm/md/lg) governs text size + padding (NOT height -- chip is inline, not a form control). Optional leading slot for an icon before the label. Pass content for rich children (wins over label). Pass removeHref for a trailing remove x rendered as a real <a href> (server-first removal, plain GET, no JS) and removeLabel for the remove link's aria-label (REQUIRED when removeHref is set; absence is an axe-core link-name violation). Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String>, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute for per-row DB values). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): no client state, no island, zero JS, CSP-clean.
docs: Copies the chip partial to your JTE root at lievit/chip.jte, reached as @template.lievit.chip(label="..."). A generic removable tag: variant drives the fill + foreground tokens (same family as badge: default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link/neutral/success/warning/danger/info); size is sm/md/lg (text + padding scale; not height-based -- chips are inline elements, not form controls). Pass label for plain text or a content slot for rich children (content wins). Pass a leading Content slot for an icon before the label. Pass removeHref to render a trailing x as a real <a href> (server-first removal, plain GET to the URL the server builds; no JS) plus removeLabel for its aria-label (REQUIRED when removeHref is set -- a missing label is an axe-core link-name violation). The template emits a dev warning comment when removeHref is set and removeLabel is absent. Extra attributes via two channels by trust: attrs is a TRUSTED raw string ($unsafe, static author-typed strings ONLY -- NEVER per-row DB values); dataAttrs is a SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes whose values are HTML-escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute. Domain meaning (which filter/entity) lives in your call, not the component. The source is yours: edit freely.
